channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Facing pressure from Trump, Costa Rica and Honduras join Panama as stopovers for foreign deportees
item.description: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica (AP) — A group of families and children hailing from Uzbekistan, China, Afghanistan, Russia and more countries climbed down the stairs of an airplane in Costa Rica's capital Thursday, the first flight of deportees from other nations Costa Rica agreed to hold in detention facilit
item.dc:creator: JAVIER CÓRDOBA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump admin violated order to unfreeze foreign aid but declines to hold officials in contempt
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration violated a court order to temporarily unfreeze foreign aid but declined to hold officials in civil contempt over the transgression. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration throws out protections from deportation for roughly half a million Haitians
item.description: The Trump administration is throwing out protections for roughly half a million Haitians that had shielded them from deportation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains said to be of a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lula says Bolsonaro’s push for amnesty means he's admitting guilt in Brazil coup plot
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said Thursday that his predecessor, Jair Bolsonaro, is acknowledging his guilt in a case in which he's accused of plotting a coup to stay in power after his 2022 electoral defeat by focusing on a campaign for amnesty.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A news conference between Zelenskyy and Trump’s Ukraine envoy is canceled amid growing tensions
item.description: A news conference that was planned to follow talks between Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and U.S. President Donald Trump’s Ukraine envoy has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China, Iran and Russia condemned by dissidents at UN watchdog's Geneva summit
item.description: Dissidents from across the globe gathered in Geneva to speak about their experiences fighting oppressive regimes. Family and friends of dissidents spoke as well, some in honor of their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis got out of bed to eat breakfast on 7th day in hospital: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis, who has been hospitalized since Feb. 14, apparently got out of bed for breakfast and is showing "slight improvement" in his medical tests, according to Vatican News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China begins repatriation from Thailand of more than 1,000 online scam workers rescued from Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An airlift carrying more than 1,000 Chinese nationals who had worked at online scam centers in eastern Myanmar began Thursday, after the rescued workers were taken across the border to Thailand and put on chartered flights to China.
item.dc:creator: JUTARAT SKULPICHETRAT and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google caught in crossfire over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Google has found itself back in the crosshairs of a geopolitical battle over President Trump’s renaming of the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America, raising fresh questions over the role technology companies can play in reinforcing political narratives.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 slain Israelis, including Shiri Bibas and her two young boys
item.description: Hamas has turned over the bodies of four slain Israeli hostages more than 500 days after the terrorist group's Oct. 7, 2023, attack in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Don Bacon says Trump 'wrong' to call Zelensky a dictator
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said President Trump was “wrong” to call Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky a dictator Wednesday and for appearing to be “standing on the side” of Russian President Vladimir Putin. “The president needs a do-over day – and start again.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel confirms 4 dead hostages who will be returned from Gaza include young family
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The remains of the youngest and one of the oldest captives seized by Hamas during the attack that ignited the war in the Gaza Strip are expected to be returned on Thursday, an Israeli official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U.S. to be “foreign terrorist organizations.” They are involved in drug trafficking, migrant smuggling and extend their territory through violence.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iranian crown prince calls for global action against Khamenei's regime
item.description: Iranian Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi demanded global action against the Islamic Republic's regime while delivering the keynote address at the Geneva Summit for Human Rights and Democracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who attacked Japan's ex-Prime Minister Kishida is convicted and sentenced to 10 years in prison
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday convicted a man who threw a homemade pipe bomb at Japan's former Prime Minister Fumio Kishida at a 2023 campaign event, sentencing him to 10 years in prison, court officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s prosecutor-general files charges against ex-President Bolsonaro over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet has filed charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro, alleging he attempted a coup to stay in office following his 2022 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases apparently in trade for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the devastated Gaza
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane over the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea; the plane did not have to alter its path to avoid a collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio makes progress with Russia after Trump promised to bring end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Macro Rubio said first step in ending the war in Ukraine is reestablishing diplomacy with Russia by reopening embassies in Moscow and Washington, as EU watches with concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has bilateral pneumonia but remains in good spirits, the Vatican says
item.description: Nevertheless the pope is in good spirits and is grateful for the prayers for his recovery, Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former USAID official warns China is already looking to fill void left by paused programs
item.description: China is reportedly trying to fill the voids left in several countries by the Trump administration's freeze of USAID funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta plane crash: Passengers live after escaping ‘upside down’ jet
item.description: Air traffic control audio captured the moment officials realized Delta Flight 4819 was burning on the tarmac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation
